title: Crispy Egyptian Lamb Casserole
description: A savory, slowcooked lamb casserole topped with a golden, crispy breadcrumb crust, bursting with classic Egyptian spices.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until aromatic.
Add the lamb mince to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon. Cook until browned, about 7 minutes.
Pour in the tomato paste, crushed tomatoes, and chicken broth, stirring to combine.
Season the mixture with cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, cinnamon, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Simmer the sauce for 5 minutes to meld the flavors.
Transfer the lamb mixture to a greased slow‑cooker or casserole dish.
Cover with a thin layer of breadcrumbs.
Set the slow‑cooker to low and cook for 90 minutes, or bake in a 180°C oven for 90 minutes.
During the last 10 minutes, increase the heat or turn the broiler to brown the breadcrumb topping until crisp.
Remove from heat, sprinkle with fresh parsley, and serve hot.
